articles.title: ONE VIRTUAL STAGE
articles.summary: Product teams are shipping AI-powered features faster than ever. But how do you experiment, release, and optimize AI experiences without increasing risk?

articles.title: PAST, PRESENT, AND FUTURE OF PMS
articles.summary: PMs are not “dying” but changing in scope depending on the organization. This article shares a framework for PMs' past, present, and future across several areas, with special emphasis on how AI and AGI pressure will reshape responsibilities.

articles.title: 4 NEW WAYS TO GO FROM IDEA TO PRODUCT WITH AI TOOLS
articles.summary: AI tools are changing product development workflows. New approaches move teams from early ideas to building and production, showing what changes across the product lifecycle.

articles.title: WHY AI PRODUCTS LOSE USERS AFTER THE FIRST TRY - HOW TO DESIGN FOR ADOPTION
articles.summary: AI products often lose users not because the model is weak, but because adoption design decisions are missing or wrong. There are four common failure modes: activation cliffs, a trust gap, broken correction loops, and an agent autonomy ceiling.

articles.title: I THINK ANTHROPIC AND OPENAI HAVE FOUND PRODUCT-MARKET FIT
articles.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI have achieved PMF for enterprise coding/general-purpose agent tools. The shift is driven by real enterprise demand for products like Claude Code/Cowork and Codex, despite thin “AI cost failure” anecdotes.

articles.title: OPENPROSE: GIVE YOUR AGENTS SLAS THAT HOLD OVER TIME
articles.summary: Tired of babysitting agents? With OpenProse [11], you define your outcome ("Inbox at zero by 5pm") - the runtime executes, watches for drift, and pings you when it's done. Write your goal once in plain English, let the system keeps it true. To start, simply open terminal

articles.title: TO REACH YOUR BIG GOAL, YOU NEED TO SELL WHERE YOU'RE HEADING NEXT
articles.summary: To achieve a big long-term goal, teams must “sell Point C”- a concrete, vivid next destination they will reach in the next 12–24 months - rather than settling for “continue existing.” This should be a strategic, fundable milestone aligned with mission, vision, strategy, and anchored in the needs of the community the team serves.

articles.title: HOW USER SEGMENTATION, RATHER THAN PERSONAS, HELPS YOU GET DESIGN BUY-IN
articles.summary: User segmentation gets more design buy-in than relying on personas alone. Personas often get dismissed, and there are four segmentation elements to reinforce personas: total audience size, time period/seasonality, device/tech context, and new vs. returning customer.

articles.title: SLACK AI: THE PATH TO MULTI-CLOUD
articles.summary: Slack evolved its AI infrastructure from self-managed AWS SageMaker to managed Amazon Bedrock to solve scaling and latency issues. By moving to Bedrock, Slack reduced engineering overhead and improved efficiency through Provisioned Throughput and on-demand capacity.

articles.title: AI APPS AREN'T DEAD, BUT THIN WRAPPERS ARE
articles.summary: AI labs will own many horizontal AI tools, but startups can still win in complex vertical workflows. The strongest AI app companies will own the system of work, including customer data, governance, integrations, and measurable business outcomes.

articles.title: HOW MUCH CAN YOU TRUST AN LLM TO TELL YOU WHAT YOUR CUSTOMERS WANT?
articles.summary: Product leaders shouldn't outsource customer thinking to LLMs.

articles.title: HOW CALIBRATION SESSIONS MESS WITH YOUR PERFORMANCE REVIEW
articles.summary: At senior levels, promotions depend on more than your manager's support.
